20.21: He said to her, "What do you want?" She replied, "Order that these two sons of mine, one at your right hand and the other at your left, should sit in your kingdom."«
20.22: Jesus said to them, «You do not know what you are asking. Can you drink the cup that I am about to drink?» “We can,” they said to him.
20.23: He answered them, «You will indeed drink from my cup, but to sit at my right or my left is not mine to grant, except to those for whom it has been prepared by my Father.»
20.24: Having heard this, the other ten were indignant against the two brothers. To give one's life as a ransom for the multitude
20.25: But Jesus called them together and said, «You know that the rulers of the nations lord it over them, and that the great ones exercise authority over them.
20.26: It will not be so among you, but whoever wants to become great among you must become your servant,
20.27: And whoever wants to be first among you must become your slave.
20.28: This is why the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.» Miracle for two blind men of Jericho
20.29: As they left Jericho, a large crowd followed them.
20.30: And behold, two blind men, who were sitting by the roadside, hearing that Jesus was passing by, began to cry out, «Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!»
20.31: The crowd rebuked them to silence them, but they shouted all the louder: «Lord, Son of David, have mercy on us!»
20.32: Jesus stopped, called them, and said, «What do you want me to do for you?
20.33: »Lord,” they said to him, “open our eyes.”
20.34: Moved with compassion, Jesus touched their eyes, and immediately they regained their sight and followed him.

21.1: When they approached Jerusalem and arrived at Bethphage, near the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two of his disciples,
21.2: saying to them, «Go to the village ahead of you, you will immediately find a donkey tied up and a colt with her, untie them, and bring them to me.
21.3: And if anyone says anything to you, reply that the Lord needs them, and they will be let go immediately.»
21.4: And this happened, so that the word of the prophet might be fulfilled:
21.5: «Tell the daughter of Zion, »See, your king is coming to you, gentle and seated on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of her who bears the yoke.’”
21.6: So the disciples went and did what Jesus had commanded them. Jesus' triumphant entry into Jerusalem
21.7: They brought the donkey and the colt, put their cloaks on them, and made them sit on them.
21.8: The people in large numbers spread their cloaks along the road, others cut branches from trees and strewn them across the road.
21.9: And all these people, in front of Jesus and behind him, shouted: «Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest heaven!»
21.10: When he entered Jerusalem, the whole city was in an uproar, people were saying, "Who is it?"«
21.11: And the people answered, "This is Jesus the prophet, from Nazareth in Galilee."« Jesus drives the merchants out of the temple
21.12: When Jesus entered the temple, he drove out all those who were buying and selling there, overturning the tables of the money changers and the seats of those selling doves.,
21.13: and said to them, «It is written: »My house shall be called a house of prayer,’ but you have made it a den of robbers.”
21.14: Blind and lame people came to him in the temple, and he healed them.
21.15: But the chief priests and the scribes, seeing the miracles he performed, and the children who cried out in the temple, saying, "Hosanna to the son of David," were indignant,
21.16: And they said to him, «Do you hear what they are saying?» “Yes,” Jesus replied, “have you never read: ‘From the lips of children and infants you have prepared a hymn of praise for yourselves’?”
21.17: And having left them there, he went out of the city and headed towards Bethany, where he spent the night in the open air. Jesus curses a fig tree
21.18: The next morning, as he was returning to the city, he was hungry.
21.19: Seeing a fig tree by the roadside, he approached it, but found nothing but leaves on it, and he said to it, "May no fruit ever be born from you again." And at once the fig tree withered.
21.20: At this sight, the disciples said in amazement, "How did it dry up in an instant?"« The power of prayer
21.21: Jesus answered them, «Truly I tell you, if you have faith and do not doubt, not only will you do as was done to the fig tree, but also you will say to this mountain, ‘Go, throw yourself into the sea,’ and it will be done.
21.22: Whatever you ask for in prayer with faith, you will receive.»
21.23: When he entered the temple and was teaching, the chief priests and the elders approached him and said, «By what authority are you doing these things, and who gave you this authority?»
21.24: Jesus answered them, «I too will ask you a question, and if you answer it, I will tell you by what right I do these things:
21.25: "Where did John's baptism come from, from heaven or from men?" But they were considering this among themselves:
21.26: «If we answer, »From heaven,’ he will say to us, ‘Why then did you not believe in him?’ And if we answer, ‘From men,’ we have to fear the people, for everyone considers John to be a prophet.”
21.27: They answered Jesus, «We do not know. And I,» Jesus said, “cannot tell you by what authority I do these things.” Parable of the Two Sons
21.28: «But what do you think? A man had two sons, and he went to the first and said, ‘My son, go and work today in my vineyard.
21.29: He replied: I don't want to, but then, moved by remorse, he went.
21.30: Then, addressing the other, he gave him the same command. The latter replied: I will go, lord, and he did not go.
21.31: Which of the two did the will of his father? The first, they told him. Then Jesus said, «Truly I tell you, the tax collectors and prostitutes are entering the kingdom of God ahead of you.
21.32: For John came to you in the way of righteousness, and you did not believe him, but the tax collectors and prostitutes did believe him; and you, having seen this, have not yet repented to believe him. Parable of the Murderous Vineyard Workers
21.33: «Listen to another parable. There was a family man who planted a vineyard. He put a hedge around it, dug a winepress in it, and built a tower. Then he rented it to some tenants and went on a journey.
21.34: When the time for harvest came, he sent his servants to the tenants to receive the produce of his vineyard.
21.35: The vineyard workers seized his servants, beat one, killed another, and stoned the third.
21.36: He sent other servants again, in greater numbers than the first time, and they treated them the same way.
21.37: Finally he sent them his son, saying: they will respect my son.
21.38: But when the tenants saw the son, they said to one another, “This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, and we’ll have his inheritance.”.
21.39: And having seized him, they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him.
21.40: Now, when the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenants?»
21.41: They replied, "He will strike these wretches without pity, and rent his vineyard to other tenants, who will give him the fruit in due season."« The kingdom of God will be taken from you.
21.42: Jesus said to them, «Have you never read in the Scriptures: ‘The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone?’ The Lord has done this, and it is marvelous in our eyes.
21.43: That is why I tell you that the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a people who will produce its fruits.
21.44: Anyone who falls on this stone will be broken to pieces, and anyone on whom it falls will be crushed.»
21.45: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these parables, they understood that Jesus was speaking about them.
21.46: And they sought to seize him, but they feared the people, who regarded him as a prophet.

22.1: Jesus, speaking again, addressed them in parables, saying:
22.2: «The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding banquet for his son.
22.3: He sent his servants to call those who had been invited to the wedding, but they refused to come.
22.4: He sent out other servants, saying, “Tell those who have been invited: I have prepared my feast; my oxen and fattened animals have been slaughtered, and everything is ready. Come to the wedding feast.”.
22.5: But they paid no attention, and went their separate ways, one to his field, the other to his business,
22.6: and the others seized the servants, and after insulting them, they killed them.
22.7: The king, having learned of this, became angry, he sent his armies, exterminated these murderers and burned their city.
22.8: Then he said to his servants: the wedding feast is ready, but those I invited were not worthy.
22.9: Go to the crossroads, and invite everyone you find to the wedding feast.
22.10: These servants, going out along the roads, gathered together all those they found, good or bad, and the wedding hall was filled with guests.
22.11: The king entered to see those who were at the table and, having noticed a man there who was not wearing a wedding robe,
22.12: He said to him, "My friend, how did you get in here without a wedding dress?" And the man remained speechless.
22.13: Then the king said to his servants, “Tie his hands and feet, and throw him into the outer darkness; there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth there.”.
22.14: Because many are called, but few are chosen.» Give unto Caesar what is Caesar's
22.15: Then the Pharisees, having withdrawn, conspired to catch Jesus in his words.
22.16: And they sent some of their disciples, along with some Herodians, to tell him, «Teacher, we know that you are a man of truth and that you teach the way of God in truth, not caring about anyone, because you pay no attention to the appearance of men.
22.17: Tell us, then, what you think: Is it permissible, or not, to pay tribute to Caesar?»
22.18: Jesus, knowing their malice, said to them, «Hypocrites, why are you testing me?
22.19: "Show me the tribute coin." They presented him with a denarius.
22.20: And Jesus said to them, «Whose image is this and whose inscription?”
22.21: »From Caesar,« they told him. Then Jesus replied, »Give back to Caesar what is Caesar’s, and to God what is God’s.”
22.22: This answer filled them with admiration, and, leaving him, they went away. The Sadducees try to trap Jesus
22.23: On the same day, some Sadducees, who deny the resurrection, came to him and asked him this question:
22.24: «Master, Moses said: If a man dies and leaves no children, let his brother marry his wife and raise up children for his brother.
22.25: Now, there were seven brothers among us; the first took a wife and died, and since he had no children, he left his wife to his brother.
22.26: The same thing happened to the second, then to the third, up to the seventh.
22.27: After all of them, the woman also died.
22.28: At the time of the resurrection, whose wife will she be of the seven brothers? For they all had her.»
22.29: Jesus answered them, «You are mistaken, because you do not understand the Scriptures or the power of God.
22.30: For at the resurrection men have neither wives nor husbands, but are like the angels of God in heaven.
22.31: As for the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what God said to you, in these words:
22.32: »I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? Yet God is not the God of the dead, but of the living.”
22.33: And the people, listening to him, were filled with admiration for his doctrine. Two commandments that sum it all up
22.34: The Pharisees, having learned that Jesus had silenced the Sadducees, gathered together.
22.35: And one of them, a doctor of the law, asked him, to test him:
22.36: «"Teacher, which is the greatest commandment in the Law?"»
22.37: Jesus said to him, «You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your mind.
22.38: This is the greatest and first commandment.
22.39: The second is like it: You shall love your neighbor as yourself.
22.40: To these two commandments are attached all the Law and the Prophets.» The Divinity of the Messiah
22.41: When the Pharisees had gathered together, Jesus asked them this question:
22.42: «What is your opinion about the Christ? Whose son is he?» They answered him, «The son of David.»
22.43: «How then,” he said to them, “does David, inspired from on high, call him Lord, saying:
22.44: The Lord said to my Lord, "Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet?"
22.45: If David calls him Lord, how can he be his son?»
22.46: No one could answer him, and from that day on, no one dared to question him again.

23.1: Then Jesus, addressing the people and his disciples, spoke thus:
23.2: «"The Scribes and the Pharisees sit in Moses' chair.".
23.3: Do and observe all that they tell you, but do not imitate their works, for they say, and do not do.
23.4: They tie heavy, difficult-to-bear burdens together and place them on men's shoulders, but they do not want to lift a finger to move them.
23.5: They do all their actions to be seen by men, wearing larger phylacteries and longer fringes.
23.6: They love the first place at feasts, the best seats in the synagogues.,
23.7: greetings in public places, and being called Rabbi by men.
23.8: As for you, do not call yourselves Rabbi, for you have only one Teacher, and you are all brothers.
23.9: And do not call anyone on earth 'Father,' for you have one Father, and he is in heaven.
23.10: Nor should anyone call you Master, for you have only one Master, the Christ.
23.11: The greatest among you will be your servant.
23.12: But whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted. The Pharisees do not enter heaven
23.13: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, for you shut the kingdom of heaven in people's faces. You yourselves do not enter, nor do you allow those who come to enter.
23.14: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For under the guise of long prayers you devour widows' houses. Therefore you will receive a greater condemnation.
23.15: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you travel across seas and land to make a single proselyte, and when he becomes one, you make him twice as much a son of hell as yourselves.
23.16: Woe to you, blind guides, who say: If a man swears by the temple, it is nothing, but if he swears by the gold of the temple, he is bound.
23.17: You fools and blind men, which is greater, gold, or the temple that sanctifies gold?
23.18: And again: If a man swears by the altar, it is nothing, but if he swears by the gift that is placed on the altar, he is bound.
23.19: Blind men, which is greater, the offering, or the altar that sanctifies the offering?
23.20: Therefore, whoever swears by the altar swears by the altar and by everything on it,
23.21: And he who swears by the temple, swears by the temple and by him who dwells in it.,
23.22: And he who swears by heaven swears by the throne of God and by him who sits on it.
23.23: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! You tithe mint, dill, and cumin, and neglect the weightier matters of the law: justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These you ought to have done, without neglecting the others. Swallow the camel and filter the midge
23.24: Blind guides, who filter out the gnat, and swallow the camel.
23.25: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you clean the outside of the cup and of the dish, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence.
23.26: Blind Pharisee, first clean the inside of the cup and the dish, so that the outside also may be clean. Woe to you Pharisees!
23.27: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like whitewashed tombs, which outwardly appear beautiful, but within are full of dead people's bones and all kinds of decay.
23.28: So you, on the outside, appear righteous to men, but inwardly you are full of hypocrisy and iniquity.
23.29: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites, who build the tombs of the prophets and adorn the monuments of the righteous!,
23.30: and who say: If we had lived in the days of our fathers, we would not have been their accomplices in shedding the blood of the prophets.
23.31: Thus you bear witness against yourselves that you are the sons of those who killed the prophets.
23.32: Fill up, then, the measure of your fathers.
23.33: Snakes, brood of vipers, how will you avoid being condemned to hell?
23.34: Therefore, I am sending you prophets, wise men, and teachers. Some of them you will kill and crucify, others you will beat with clubs in your synagogues, and you will pursue them from town to town.
23.35: so that on you may fall all the innocent blood shed on earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah, son of Barachiah, whom you killed between the temple and the altar.
23.36: Truly I tell you, all this will come upon this generation. Your temple is left deserted.
23.37: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those sent to you, how often I have longed to gather your children together, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, and you were not willing.
23.38: Behold, your temple is left to you deserted.
23.39: For I tell you, you will not see me again until you say, »Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.’”

24.1: As Jesus was leaving the temple, his disciples approached him to point out its buildings.
24.2: But he answered them, «Do you see all these buildings? Truly I tell you, not one stone here will be left on another; every one will be thrown down.»
24.3: When he sat down on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him, and they were alone with him. They asked, «Tell us, when will these things happen, and what will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the age?»
24.4: Jesus answered them, «Beware lest anyone deceive you.
24.5: For many will come in my name, saying, 'I am the Christ,' and they will deceive many.
24.6: You will hear of wars and rumors of wars; do not be troubled by them, for these things must happen, but the end is not yet. Plagues, famines and earthquakes
24.7: Nation will rise against nation, kingdom against kingdom, and there will be plagues, famines, and earthquakes in various places.
24.8: All of this will only be the beginning of the pain.
24.9: Then they will hand you over to be tortured and put to death, and you will be hated by all nations because of my name.
24.10: Then many will fail, they will betray and hate each other.
24.11: And many false prophets will arise and deceive a great number of people.
24.12: And because of the increasing progress of iniquity, the charity of many will grow cold. Whoever perseveres to the end will be saved
24.13: But the one who perseveres to the end will be saved.
24.14: This gospel of the kingdom will be preached throughout the world as a testimony to all nations, and then the end will come.
24.15: «When you see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by the prophet Daniel, standing in the holy place (and let the reader understand),
24.16: while those in Judea flee to the mountains,
24.17: and let the one who is on the roof not come down to take what he has in his house,
24.18: and let him who is in the fields not return to get his garment.
24.19: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are breastfeeding in those days.
24.20: Pray that your escape does not occur in winter, nor on a Sabbath day.,
24.21: for then there will be such great distress as has not been from the beginning of the world until now, and never will be again. These days will be shortened because of the elected officials.
24.22: And if those days were not shortened, no one would escape; but for the sake of the elect, those days will be shortened.
24.23: So if someone tells you, 'Here is the Christ,' or 'There he is,' do not believe it.
24.24: For false Christs and false prophets will arise and perform great signs and wonders, so as to deceive, if possible, even the elect.
24.25: There you have it, I told you so.
24.26: So if they tell you, 'Here he is in the desert,' do not go out; or, 'Here he is in the innermost part of the house,' do not believe it.
24.27: For as lightning comes from the east and flashes to the west, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
24.28: Wherever the corpse is, there the vultures will gather. The sign of the Son of Man will appear
24.29: Immediately after the days of their affliction, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light, and the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
24.30: Then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in the sky, and all the tribes of the earth will beat their breasts, and they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of the sky with great power and great majesty.
24.31: And he will send out his angels with a loud trumpet call, and they will gather his elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other. A comparison taken from the fig tree
24.32: «"Listen to a comparison taken from the fig tree. As soon as its branches become tender, and it puts out its leaves, you know that summer is near.
24.33: So when you see all these things, you will know that the Son of Man is near, that he is at the door.
24.34: I tell you the truth, this generation will not pass away until all these things happen.
24.35: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
24.36: As for the day and the hour, no one knows them, not even the angels in heaven, but only the Father.
24.37: As were the days of Noah, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
24.38: For in the days before the flood, people were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, up to the day Noah entered the ark,
24.39: And they knew nothing until the flood came and swept them all away: so it will be at the coming of the Son of Man.
24.40: So, of two men who will be in a field, one will be taken, the other left,
24.41: of two women who will be ground at the millstone, one will be taken, the other left. Be vigilant, be ready
24.42: Therefore, keep watch, because you do not know when your Lord will come.
24.43: Know this well: if the father of the family knew what time the thief was coming, he would be vigilant and would not let his house be broken into.
24.44: Therefore you also must be ready, for the Son of Man will come at an hour when you do not expect him.
24.45: Who then is the faithful and wise servant, whom his master has put in charge of his household, to give them their food at the proper time?
24.46: Blessed is that servant whom his master finds so doing when he returns.
24.47: Truly I tell you, he will establish it over all his possessions.
24.48: But if that servant is wicked and says to himself, “My master is taking a long time to come,”,
24.49: He began to beat his companions, and to eat and drink with people who were fond of wine.,
24.50: The master of that servant will come on a day when he does not expect him, and at an hour he does not know.,
24.51: And he will have him torn with blows, and assign him his place with the hypocrites: there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth there.»

25.1: «Then the kingdom of heaven will be like ten virgins who took their lamps and went to meet the bridegroom.”.
25.2: There were five who were crazy and five who were wise.
25.3: The five foolish women, having taken their lamps, did not take any oil with them.,
25.4: But the wise men took oil in their vessels along with their lamps.
25.5: As the husband was late in coming, they all became drowsy and fell asleep.
25.6: In the middle of the night, a cry went up: Here is the bridegroom coming, go out to meet him.
25.7: Then all the virgins got up and prepared their lamps.
25.8: And the foolish ones said to the wise ones, “Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out.”.
25.9: The wise men replied: Lest there not be enough for us and for you, go rather to those who sell it, and buy some for yourselves.
25.10: But while they were going to buy some, the bridegroom arrived, and those who were ready went in with him to the wedding hall, and the door was shut.
25.11: Later, the other virgins also came, saying, "Lord, Lord, open to us.".
25.12: He answered them, “Truly I tell you, I do not know you.”.
25.13: Therefore, keep watch, because you do not know the day or the hour. Parable of the Talents
25.14: For it will be like a man going on a journey, who called his servants and entrusted his possessions to them.
25.15: To one he gave five talents, to another two, to another one, according to each one's ability, and he departed immediately.
25.16: The one who had received five talents went away and put them to good use, and gained five more.
25.17: Similarly, the one who had received two, won two more.
25.18: But the one who had received only one went and dug a hole in the ground and hid his master's money there.
25.19: A long time later, the master of these servants returned and made them give an account.
25.20: The one who had received five talents came forward and presented him with five more, saying, “Master, you entrusted me with five talents; see, here are five more that I have gained.”.
25.21: His master said to him, “Well done, good and faithful servant; because you have been faithful over a little, I will set you over much. Enter into the joy of your master.”.
25.22: The one who had received two talents also came and said, “Master, you entrusted me with two talents; see, I have gained two more.”.
25.23: His master said to him, “Well done, good and faithful servant; because you have been faithful over a little, I will set you over much. Enter into the joy of your master.”.
25.24: Approaching in turn, the one who had received only one talent said, “Master, I knew you to be a hard man, reaping where you have not sown, and gathering where you have not winnowed.”.
25.25: I was afraid, and I hid your talent in the ground; here it is, I am returning to you what is yours.
25.26: His master answered him, “You wicked, lazy servant! You knew that I reap where I have not sown, and gather where I have not winnowed?”,
25.27: So you had to take my money to the bankers, and, on my return, I would have withdrawn what belonged to me with interest.
25.28: Take that talent away from him, and give it to the one who has ten.
25.29: For to him who has, more will be given, and he will have abundance; but from him who does not have, even what he has will be taken away.
25.30: And throw that useless servant into the outer darkness: there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth. Jesus will separate the sheep from the goats
25.31: When the Son of Man comes in his glory, and all the angels with him, he will sit on his glorious throne.
25.32: And, with all the nations gathered before him, he will separate them one from another, as a shepherd separates the sheep from the goats.
25.33: And he will place the sheep on his right, and the goats on his left. Come, you blessed of my Father!
25.34: Then the King will say to those on his right, “Come, you who are blessed by my Father; take your inheritance, the kingdom prepared for you from the beginning of the world.”.
25.35: For I was hungry and you gave me food, I was thirsty and you gave me drink, I was a stranger and you welcomed me,
25.36: Naked, and you clothed me; sick, and you visited me; in prison, and you came to me.
25.37: The righteous will answer him, “Lord, when did we see you hungry and feed you, or thirsty and give you something to drink?”
25.38: When did we see you a stranger, and take you in, naked, and clothe you?
25.39: When did we see you sick or in prison, and come to you?
25.40: And the King will answer them, “Truly I tell you, whatever you did for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you did for me.”. Cursed ones, go to the eternal fire
25.41: Then, addressing those on his left, he will say: Depart from me, you cursed, into the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels.
25.42: For I was hungry, and you gave me nothing to eat, I was thirsty, and you gave me nothing to drink,
25.43: I was a stranger, and you did not take me in; naked, and you did not clothe me; sick and in prison, and you did not visit me.
25.44: Then they too will say to him, “Lord, when did we see you hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison, and did not minister to you?”
25.45: And he will answer them, “Truly I tell you, whatever you did not do for one of the least of these, you did not do for me.”.
25.46: And these will go away to eternal torment, but the righteous to eternal life.»

26.1: When Jesus had finished all these sayings, he said to his disciples:
26.2: «You know that the Passover is in two days, and that the Son of Man is going to be handed over to be crucified.»
26.3: Then the Princes of the priests and the Elders of the people gathered in the court of the high priest, called Caiaphas,
26.4: And they plotted how they might seize Jesus by trickery and kill him.
26.5: «"But," they said, "it must not be during the festival, lest there be some tumult among the people."» A woman pours perfume on Jesus
26.6: While Jesus was in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper,
26.7: A woman approached him with an alabaster vase containing very expensive perfume, and while he was at the table, she poured the perfume over his head.
26.8: Seeing this, the disciples said indignantly, "What good is this loss?"
26.9: We could have sold this perfume at a very high price and given the proceeds to the poor.»
26.10: Jesus, having noticed this, said to them, «Why are you bothering this woman? She has done a good thing for me.
26.11: For you always have the poor with you, but you do not always have me.
26.12: By spreading this perfume on my body, she did it for my burial.
26.13: Truly I tell you, wherever this gospel is preached throughout the world, what she did will be recounted in her memory.» Judas's betrayal
26.14: Then one of the Twelve, called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests,
26.15: and said to them, "What are you willing to give me and I will deliver it to you?" And they counted out thirty pieces of silver to him.
26.16: From that moment on, he sought a favorable opportunity to betray Jesus.
26.17: On the first day of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus and asked, «Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?»
26.18: Jesus answered them, «Go into the city to a certain man and tell him, »The Teacher says: My time is near; I will celebrate the Passover with my disciples at your house.’”
26.19: The disciples did as Jesus had commanded them, and they prepared the Passover.
26.20: When evening came, he sat down to eat with the Twelve. It would have been better if Judas had not been born
26.21: While they were eating, he said, "Truly I tell you, one of you will betray me."«
26.22: They were deeply saddened, and each one began to say to him, "Is it I, Lord?"«
26.23: He replied: "Whoever has dipped their hand into the dish with me will betray me.".
26.24: The Son of Man will go as it is written about him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been better for him if that man had not been born.»
26.25: Judas, who betrayed him, spoke up and said, "Is it I, Master?" "You have said so," replied Jesus. This is my body, this is my blood
26.26: During the meal, Jesus took the bread, and after saying a blessing, he broke it and gave it to his disciples, saying, «Take and eat; this is my body.»
26.27: Then he took the cup, and after giving thanks, he gave it to them, saying, «Drink from it, all of you:
26.28: for this is my blood, the blood of the new covenant, which is shed for many for the forgiveness of sins.
26.29: I tell you, I will not drink again of this fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new with you in my Father's kingdom.» Jesus prophesies Peter's denial
26.30: After the singing of the hymn, they went to the Mount of Olives.
26.31: Then Jesus said to them, «This very night I will cause all of you to stumble, for it is written: ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.’.
26.32: But after I have risen, I will go ahead of you to Galilee.»
26.33: Peter, speaking up, said to him, «Even if you cause everyone to stumble, you never will cause me to stumble.»
26.34: Jesus said to him, «Truly I tell you, this very night, before the rooster crows, you will deny me three times.»
26.35: Peter answered him, «Even if I have to die with you, I will not deny you.» And all the other disciples said the same. Agony in Gethsemane
26.36: Then Jesus went with them to a place called Gethsemane, and he said to his disciples, «Sit here while I go over there and pray.»
26.37: Having taken with him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, he began to feel sorrow and anguish. My soul is sad unto death
26.38: And he said to them, «My soul is overwhelmed with sorrow to the point of death; stay here and keep watch with me.»
26.39: And going a little farther, he bowed down with his face to the ground, praying and saying, «My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass from me. Yet not as I will, but as you will.»
26.40: He then came to his disciples, and finding them asleep, he said to Peter, «So you could not watch with me for one hour.
26.41: Watch and pray so that you will not fall into temptation. The spirit is willing, but the flesh is weak.»
26.42: He withdrew a second time and prayed thus: "My Father, if this cup cannot pass away unless I drink it, may your will be done."«
26.43: When he came again, he found them still asleep, for their eyes were heavy.
26.44: He left them, and went away to pray for the third time, saying the same words.
26.45: Then he returned to his disciples and said to them, «Sleep now and rest, for the hour is near when the Son of Man will be delivered into the hands of sinners.
26.46: "Get up, come on, the one who betrays me is near here."» Jesus' arrest because of Judas
26.47: He was still speaking when Judas, one of the Twelve, arrived, and with him a large troop of men armed with swords and clubs, sent by the chief priests and the elders of the people.
26.48: The traitor had given them this sign: "The one I kiss is him, arrest him."«
26.49: And immediately, approaching Jesus, he said, "Greetings, Master," and he kissed him.
26.50: Jesus said to him, «Friend, why are you here?» At the same time, they came forward, laid hands on Jesus, and seized him.
26.51: And behold, one of those who were with Jesus, drawing his sword, struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
26.52: Then Jesus said to him, «Put your sword back in its place, for all who draw the sword will perish by the sword.”. May the oracles of the prophets be fulfilled.
26.53: Do you think I cannot pray to my Father right now, who would give me more than twelve legions of angels?
26.54: How then will the Scriptures be fulfilled, which testify that it must happen this way?»
26.55: At the same time, Jesus said to the crowd, «Have you come out as to a thief, with swords and clubs to arrest me? Every day I sat among you in the temple courts teaching, and you did not seize me,
26.56: But all this was done so that the words of the prophets might be fulfilled.» Then all the disciples deserted him and fled.
26.57: Those who had arrested Jesus took him to Caiaphas, the high priest, where the scribes and the elders of the people had gathered.
26.58: Peter followed him at a distance to the courtyard of the high priest, entered, and sat with the servants to see the end.
26.59: However, the chief priests and the entire council were seeking false testimony against Jesus in order to have him put to death.,
26.60: And they found none, although several false witnesses had come forward. Finally, two came.
26.61: who said, "This man said, 'I can destroy the temple of God and rebuild it in three days.'"« Jesus declares his divinity
26.62: The high priest stood up and said to Jesus, «Have you nothing to say to the charges these men are bringing against you?»
26.63: Jesus remained silent. And the high priest said to him, «I adjure you by the living God, tell us if you are the Christ, the Son of God?»
26.64: Jesus answered him, «You have said so; moreover, I tell you, from now on you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of the Almighty and coming on the clouds of heaven.»
26.65: Then the high priest tore his clothes, saying, «He has blasphemed! What further need do we have of witnesses? You have just heard his blasphemy!”
26.66: "What do you think?" They replied, "He deserves to die."»
26.67: Then they spat in his face, and punched him, others slapped him,
26.68: saying, "Christ, guess who hit you."« Peter's Denial and Tears
26.69: However, Peter was outside, sitting in the courtyard. A servant girl approached him and said, "You also were with Jesus of Galilee."«
26.70: But he denied it in front of everyone, saying, "I don't know what you mean."«
26.71: As he was heading towards the vestibule to leave, another servant girl saw him and said to those who were there, "This man was also with Jesus of Nazareth."«
26.72: And Peter denied it a second time under oath: "I do not know this man."«
26.73: Shortly afterward, those who were there approached Peter and said to him, "Surely you are one of them, for your very speech gives you away."«
26.74: Then he began to curse and swear that he did not know this man. Immediately the rooster crowed.
26.75: And Peter remembered the word that Jesus had spoken to him: «Before the rooster crows, you will deny me three times,» and he went out and wept bitterly.

27.1: Early in the morning, all the chief priests and the elders of the people held a council against Jesus to put him to death.
27.2: And, having bound him, they led him away and handed him over to the governor Pontius Pilate.
27.3: Then Judas, who had betrayed him, seeing that he was condemned, was moved with repentance, and returned the thirty pieces of silver to the Chief Priests and the Elders,
27.4: saying, "I have sinned by betraying innocent blood." They replied, "What is that to us? That's your problem."«
27.5: Then, having thrown the silver coins into the Sanctuary, he withdrew and went and hanged himself.
27.6: But the chief priests gathered up the money and said, "It is not permitted to put it into the sacred treasury, since it is blood money."«
27.7: And, after consulting among themselves, they used this money to buy the Potter's field for the burial of strangers.
27.8: That is why this field is still called the Field of Blood today.
27.9: Then was fulfilled the word of the prophet Jeremiah: «They received thirty pieces of silver, the price of him whose value the children of Israel had determined,
27.10: and they gave them for the potter's field, as the Lord commanded me.» «"Are you the king of the Jews?"»
27.11: Jesus appeared before the governor, and the governor questioned him, saying, «Are you the king of the Jews?» Jesus answered him, «You say so.»
27.12: But he did not respond to the accusations of the Princes of the Priests and the Elders.
27.13: Then Pilate said to him, "Do you not hear how many things they are accusing you of?"«
27.14: But he did not answer any of his grievances, so the governor was greatly astonished.
27.15: At each Easter festival, the governor used to release a prisoner, the one the crowd demanded.
27.16: Now they had a famous prisoner at that time, named Barabbas.
27.17: Pilate, having summoned the people, said to him, «Which one do you want me to deliver to you, Barabbas or Jesus who is called Christ?»
27.18: Because he knew that it was out of envy that they had handed Jesus over.
27.19: While he was sitting on his tribunal, his wife sent word to him: "Let there be nothing between you and that righteous man, for I have been greatly troubled today in a dream because of him."« The Princes of the Priests persuade the people
27.20: But the chief priests and the elders persuaded the people to ask for Barabbas and to have Jesus killed.
27.21: The governor addressed them, saying, "Which of the two do you want me to release for you?" They replied, "Barabbas."«
27.22: Pilate said to them, «What then shall I do with Jesus, who is called Christ?»
27.23: They answered him, "Let him be crucified!" The governor said to them, "What evil has he done?" And they shouted all the louder, "Let him be crucified!"«
27.24: Pilate, seeing that he was gaining nothing, but that the uproar was increasing, took water and washed his hands in front of the people, saying, «I am innocent of this righteous man’s blood; you are the ones who must answer for it.»
27.25: And all the people said, "His blood be on us and on our children."« Flagellation and Crowning with Thorns
27.26: Then he released Barabbas to them, and after having Jesus flogged, he handed him over to be crucified.
27.27: The governor's soldiers took Jesus into the Praetorium, and they gathered the whole cohort around him.
27.28: Having stripped him of his clothes, they threw a scarlet cloak over him.
27.29: They twisted together a crown of thorns and placed it on his head. They also put a reed in his right hand and, kneeling before him, mocked him, saying, «Hail, King of the Jews.»
27.30: They also spat in his face, and taking the reed, they struck his head with it.
27.31: After mocking him in this way, they took off his cloak, put his own clothes back on him, and led him away to be crucified. Stations of the Cross and Crucifixion
27.32: As they went out, they met a man from Cyrene named Simon, whom they compelled to carry Jesus' cross.
27.33: Then, having arrived at the place called Golgotha, that is, the place of the Skull,
27.34: They gave him wine mixed with gall to drink, but after tasting it, he refused to drink it.
27.35: When they had crucified him, they divided his clothes among themselves by casting lots, so that what was spoken through the Prophet might be fulfilled: «They divided my garments among themselves, and for my robe they cast lots.»
27.36: And, having sat down, they kept watch over him.
27.37: Above his head they placed a sign indicating the reason for his execution: "This is Jesus, the King of the Jews."«
27.38: At the same time, two robbers were crucified with him, one on his right and the other on his left.
27.39: And passersby insulted him, shaking their heads.
27.40: and saying, «You who destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, save yourself! If you are the Son of God, come down from the cross!»
27.41: The Princes of the Priests, along with the Scribes and the Elders, also mocked him and said:
27.42: «"He saved others, and he cannot save himself. If he is the king of Israel, let him now come down from the cross, and we will believe in him.". Jesus dies on the cross
27.43: He trusted in God; if God loves him, let him deliver him now, for he said, »I am the Son of God.”
27.44: The brigands who were crucified with him insulted him in the same way.
27.45: From the sixth hour until the ninth hour, there was darkness over all the land.
27.46: About the ninth hour, Jesus cried out with a loud voice, «Eli, Eli, lamma sabachthani, that is, my God, my God, why have you forsaken me?»
27.47: Some of those who were there, having heard it, said, "He is calling for Elijah."«
27.48: And immediately one of them ran to get a sponge, filled it with vinegar, and, having put it on the end of a reed, offered it to him to drink.
27.49: The others said, "Leave it, let's see if Elijah will come to save him."«
27.50: Jesus cried out again with a loud voice and gave up his spirit. Miracles at the death of Jesus
27.51: And behold, the veil of the sanctuary was torn in two from top to bottom; the earth shook, the rocks split,
27.52: The tombs opened, and several saints, whose bodies were lying there, were resurrected.
27.53: Having come out of their tomb, they entered the holy city after the resurrection of Jesus and appeared to many.
27.54: The centurion and those with him who were guarding Jesus, when they saw the earthquake and all that was happening, were terrified and exclaimed, «Truly this man was the Son of God!»
27.55: There were also several women there watching from a distance; they had followed Jesus from Galilee to serve him.
27.56: Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
27.57: In the evening, a rich man from Arimathea, named Joseph, who was also a disciple of Jesus, arrived.
27.58: He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. And Pilate ordered that it be given to him. Joseph of Arimathea offers his tomb to Jesus
27.59: Joseph took the body and wrapped it in a white shroud,
27.60: and placed it in the new tomb, which he had cut out of the rock for himself, and then, having rolled a large stone to the entrance of the tomb, he went away.
27.61: Now Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were there, sitting opposite the tomb.
27.62: The next day, which was Saturday, the chief priests and the Pharisees went together to Pilate,
27.63: and said to him, «Lord, we remember that this deceiver, while he was still alive, said: After three days I will rise again,
27.64: Therefore, order that his tomb be guarded until the third day, lest his disciples come and steal the body and tell the people, »He has risen from the dead.” This last deception would be worse than the first.»
27.65: Pilate replied, "You have a guard; go, guard him as you see fit."«
27.66: So they went away and secured the tomb by sealing the stone and placing guards there.

28.1: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to visit the tomb.
28.2: And behold, there was a great earthquake, for an angel of the Lord came down from heaven and rolled back the stone and sat on it.
28.3: Its appearance was like lightning, and its garment was white as snow.
28.4: At the sight of him, the guards were struck with terror and became as if dead.
28.5: And the angel, addressing the women, said: «Do not be afraid, for I know that you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
28.6: He is not here; he has risen, just as he said. Come and see the place where the Lord was laid.,
28.7: »And go quickly and tell his disciples that he has risen from the dead. Look, he is going ahead of you to Galilee; there you will see him, as I have told you.” Jesus appears to women
28.8: Immediately they came out of the tomb with fear and great joy, and they ran to tell the disciples.
28.9: And behold, Jesus stood before them and said to them, «Greetings.» And they came up and kissed his feet, and worshiped him.
28.10: Then Jesus said to them, «Do not be afraid; go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee; there they will see me.»
28.11: While they were on their way, some of the guards came into the city and told the Princes of the priests everything that had happened.
28.12: They gathered the Elders, and, having held council, they gave a large sum of money to the soldiers,
28.13: telling them, «Proclaim that his disciples came by night and took him away while you were sleeping.
28.14: And if the governor finds out, we will appease him and protect you.»
28.15: The soldiers took the money and did as they were told, and the rumor they spread is still repeated among the Jews today. Jesus mentions the Holy Trinity
28.16: The eleven disciples went to Galilee, to the mountain that Jesus had indicated to them.
28.17: When they saw him, they adored him, they who had hesitated to believe.
28.18: And Jesus came to them and said, «All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me.
28.19: Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
28.20: teaching them to observe all that I have commanded you; and behold, I am with you always, to the end of the age.»
